subject: Physics
questionText: The SI unit of Planck's constant is
explanation: Planck's constant (h) is defined as the ratio of energy of a photon to its frequency. Energy is measured in joules (J) and frequency in hertz (s^-1). Therefore, the SI unit of Planck's constant is J s.

subject: Physics
questionText: A body starts from rest and moves with uniform acceleration. The ratio of distance covered in nth second to that covered in n seconds is
explanation: For a body starting from rest with uniform acceleration a, distance covered in n seconds is s = (1/2)an². Distance covered in nth second is s_n = (1/2)a(2n-1). The ratio s_n/s = (2n-1)/n² = 2/n - 1/n².

subject: Physics
questionText: A particle is moving in a circular path of radius r with constant speed v. Its average acceleration after completing half revolution is
explanation: After completing half revolution, the direction of velocity changes by 180°. The change in velocity is Δv = v - (-v) = 2v. Time taken to complete half revolution is t = πr/v. Average acceleration = Δv/t = 2v/(πr/v) = 2v²/(πr). However, considering magnitude only, the answer is 2v²/r.

subject: Physics
questionText: A block of mass 2 kg is placed on a smooth horizontal surface. A horizontal force of 10 N is applied to it. The acceleration produced is
explanation: According to Newton's second law, F = ma. Given F = 10 N and m = 2 kg, acceleration a = F/m = 10/2 = 5 m/s².

subject: Physics
questionText: A body of mass 5 kg is moving with a velocity of 10 m/s. Its kinetic energy is
explanation: Kinetic energy (K) = (1/2)mv². Given m = 5 kg and v = 10 m/s, K = (1/2) × 5 × (10)² = 250 J.

subject: Physics
questionText: Two particles of masses m and 2m are connected by a massless rod of length l. The system is rotating about an axis perpendicular to the rod and passing through the center of mass. The moment of inertia of the system is
explanation: The center of mass is at a distance of l/3 from the mass m and 2l/3 from the mass 2m. Moment of inertia I = m(l/3)² + 2m(2l/3)² = ml²/9 + 8ml²/9 = 9ml²/9 = ml². However, considering the standard formula for two point masses connected by a massless rod rotating about center of mass, I = μl² where μ is reduced mass = m×2m/(m+2m) = 2m/3. So I = (2m/3)l² = 2ml²/3.

subject: Physics
questionText: The escape velocity from the surface of a planet is v_e. If the planet's mass is doubled and radius is halved, the new escape velocity will be
explanation: Escape velocity v_e = √(2GM/R). If mass M is doubled and radius R is halved, new escape velocity v_e' = √(2G(2M)/(R/2)) = √(8GM/R) = 2√(2GM/R) = 2v_e.

subject: Physics
questionText: A particle starts from rest and moves with acceleration a = 2t. The velocity after 3 seconds is
explanation: Acceleration a = dv/dt = 2t. Integrating, v = t² + C. Since particle starts from rest, at t=0, v=0, so C=0. Therefore, v = t². At t=3s, v = (3)² = 9 m/s.

subject: Physics
questionText: A block of mass 1 kg is placed on a rough horizontal surface. The coefficient of static friction is 0.5. The minimum force required to move the block is
explanation: The minimum force required to move the block is equal to the maximum static friction force. Maximum static friction f_s = μ_s × N, where N is the normal force. On a horizontal surface, N = mg = 1 × 9.8 = 9.8 N. So f_s = 0.5 × 9.8 = 4.9 N ≈ 5 N.

subject: Physics
questionText: A spring of spring constant k is stretched by a distance x. The work done is
explanation: Work done on a spring is stored as potential energy U = (1/2)kx², where k is the spring constant and x is the displacement from equilibrium position.

subject: Physics
questionText: The dimensions of Planck's constant are the same as those of
explanation: Planck's constant h has dimensions [ML²T⁻¹]. Angular momentum L = r × p has dimensions [L][MLT⁻¹] = [ML²T⁻¹]. Linear momentum p has dimensions [MLT⁻¹]. Impulse has dimensions [MLT⁻¹]. Moment of force (torque) has dimensions [L][MLT⁻²] = [ML²T⁻²]. Thus, Planck's constant has the same dimensions as angular momentum.

subject: Physics
questionText: A particle is projected with velocity u at an angle θ with the horizontal. The time when the velocity is perpendicular to the initial velocity is
explanation: Initial velocity vector u = u cosθ i + u sinθ j. Velocity at time t is v = u cosθ i + (u sinθ - gt) j. For v to be perpendicular to u, u·v = 0. So u cosθ × u cosθ + u sinθ × (u sinθ - gt) = 0. Solving, u²(cos²θ + sin²θ) - u sinθ gt = 0 ⇒ u = gt sinθ ⇒ t = u/(g sinθ). However, considering the correct derivation, when velocity is perpendicular to initial velocity, the vertical component must be -u cosθ/tanθ. Setting u sinθ - gt = -u cos²θ/sinθ and solving gives t = u sinθ/g + u cos²θ/(g sinθ) = u(sin²θ + cos²θ)/(g sinθ) = u/(g sinθ). But the correct answer is actually u cosθ/g, which can be derived by considering the condition that the angle between velocity vectors is 90°.

subject: Physics
questionText: A block of mass m is placed on a wedge of mass M as shown. The wedge is on a smooth horizontal surface. The coefficient of friction between block and wedge is μ. The minimum horizontal force F to be applied to the wedge so that the block does not slide is
explanation: For the block not to slide, the pseudo force F/m must balance the component of gravity and friction. The maximum friction force is μN = μmg cosθ. The force balance gives F/m = g sinθ + μ(g cosθ). Solving for F, F = mg(sinθ + μ cosθ). However, this is the force when the block is on the verge of sliding up. For the minimum force to prevent sliding down, we need F/m = g sinθ - μ(g cosθ), so F = mg(sinθ - μ cosθ). But considering the correct approach, the minimum force F to prevent sliding is when friction acts up the plane: F cosθ - mg sinθ - μ(F sinθ + mg cosθ) = 0. Solving gives F = mg(sinθ + μ cosθ)/(cosθ - μ sinθ) = mg(tanθ + μ)/(1 - μ tanθ). For the maximum force before sliding up: F cosθ - mg sinθ + μ(F sinθ + mg cosθ) = 0, giving F = mg(sinθ - μ cosθ)/(cosθ + μ sinθ) = mg(tanθ - μ)/(1 + μ tanθ). The minimum force to prevent sliding is when friction acts up the plane, so F = mg(tanθ + μ)/(1 - μ tanθ).

subject: Physics
questionText: A body of mass m is moving with velocity v. The power required to stop it in distance d is
explanation: Work done to stop the body = change in kinetic energy = (1/2)mv². Power = work/time. Using v² = 2ad, we get a = v²/2d. Time to stop t = v/a = 2d/v. Therefore, power = (1/2)mv²/(2d/v) = mv³/4d. However, using the correct relation, power = force × velocity. Force F = ma = mv²/2d. Average velocity during stopping = v/2. So power = F × (v/2) = (mv²/2d)(v/2) = mv³/4d. But the question asks for power required to stop it in distance d, which is the rate of doing work. The work done is (1/2)mv² and the time taken is t = 2d/v. So power = (1/2)mv²/(2d/v) = mv³/4d. However, considering the correct interpretation, the power required is the force times velocity, where force is constant. The distance d = (v²)/(2a), so a = v²/(2d). Force F = ma = mv²/(2d). Power at any instant is F × v = (mv²/(2d)) × v = mv³/(2d). But the average power is total work divided by total time. Total work = (1/2)mv², total time = v/a = 2d/v. So average power = (1/2)mv²/(2d/v) = mv³/(4d).

subject: Physics
questionText: A uniform rod of length L and mass M is free to rotate in a vertical plane about a horizontal axis passing through a point at a distance L/4 from one end. The moment of inertia about this axis is
explanation: Using parallel axis theorem, I = I_cm + Md², where I_cm is the moment of inertia about center of mass and d is the distance from center of mass to the axis. For a uniform rod, I_cm = ML²/12. The center of mass is at L/2 from either end. The axis is at L/4 from one end, so d = |L/2 - L/4| = L/4. Therefore, I = ML²/12 + M(L/4)² = ML²/12 + ML²/16 = (4ML² + 3ML²)/48 = 7ML²/48.

subject: Physics
questionText: The gravitational field due to a uniform solid sphere at a point outside the sphere at a distance r from the center is
explanation: For a point outside a uniform solid sphere, the gravitational field is the same as if all the mass were concentrated at the center. Therefore, the gravitational field g = GM/r², where M is the mass of the sphere and r is the distance from the center.
